claimText: The HANSA trainer-planes are associated with CSIR-National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL), which signed a technology transfer deal with a private firm for their manufacture.
evidenceExcerpt: CSIR-National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) signs technology transfer deal with a private firm to manufacture HANSA trainer-planes.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the nodal agency responsible for the development and technology transfer of the HANSA trainer aircraft.
distractorHooks: Developed by Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
Developed by Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)

claimText: The HANSA trainer aircraft is powered by an advanced fuel-efficient Rotax 912 iSc3 Sports engine.
evidenceExcerpt: Powered by an advanced fuel-efficient Rotax 912 iSc3 Sports engine.
examUse: Fact-based statement regarding the specific engine technology utilized in the HANSA trainer aircraft.
distractorHooks: Powered by an indigenous Kaveri engine
Powered by a fully electric propulsion system

claimText: The primary objective of manufacturing HANSA trainer-planes is to strengthen India's aviation industry and flying training ecosystem.
evidenceExcerpt: Aims to strengthen India's aviation industry and flying training ecosystem.
examUse: Statement-based question on the primary purpose and application of the HANSA aircraft.
distractorHooks: Designed primarily for stealth combat missions
Aims to replace commercial passenger jets in regional connectivity schemes
